headline: Trump announces Lebanon ceasefire as US blockade continues, with new Iran peace talks possible this week

sections.label: Situation Overview
sections.title: Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire, Blockade Continues
paragraphs: US President Donald Trump announced a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon beginning at 5pm ET today, marking a potential breakthrough in regional tensions. The truce comes as Israeli operations against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon had emerged as a major sticking point in broader US-Iran negotiations.

Meanwhile, the US naval blockade of Iranian ports, which began Monday, continues as part of Trump's pressure campaign. Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth warned that US forces remain ready to restart combat if Iran refuses a deal, while Pakistan intensifies diplomatic efforts with its army chief in Tehran and Prime Minister in Qatar.
key_facts: 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire announced by Trump for 5pm ET Thursday
US blockade of Iranian ports ongoing since Monday
Pakistan's army chief currently in Tehran for diplomatic talks
Over 6,000 killed in Middle East since conflict began in late February
sections.australia_relevance: Australian fuel prices remain above $2.50 per litre in some areas due to ongoing disruption to Strait of Hormuz shipping routes.

sections.label: Background
sections.title: War Origins and Current Status
paragraphs: The conflict began February 28 when the US and Israel launched strikes against Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and other officials. Iran responded by closing the Strait of Hormuz and launching missile attacks against US bases and allies in the region.

A two-week ceasefire agreed April 8 through Pakistani mediation collapsed after weekend talks failed, primarily over Iran's nuclear program. The US now blockades Iranian ports while allowing humanitarian supplies through, as Iran maintains selective control over Strait of Hormuz transits.
key_facts: War began February 28 with US-Israeli strikes on Iran
Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei killed in initial strikes
Two-week ceasefire collapsed after weekend talks in Pakistan
Iran closed Strait of Hormuz carrying 20% of global oil supply

sections.label: Diplomacy
sections.title: Peace Efforts and International Responses
paragraphs: Pakistan has emerged as the key mediator, with new in-person talks between the US and Iran reportedly possible this week. Trump claimed Iran wants to make a deal 'very badly', while Tehran insists any ceasefire must include an end to Israeli operations against Hezbollah.

The US Treasury announced fresh sanctions on more than two dozen individuals and entities involved in Iranian oil and gas exports. China reportedly agreed not to send weapons to Iran, though US intelligence indicates Beijing is preparing to deliver air defence systems within weeks.
key_facts: New US-Iran talks possible this week in Pakistan
Fresh US sanctions on Iranian oil and gas entities announced
China agreed not to send weapons to Iran but may deliver air defence systems
Iran insists Lebanon must be included in any ceasefire deal

sections.label: Economy & Energy
sections.title: Oil Markets and Australian Impact
paragraphs: Brent crude has surged past US$111 per barrel, with Australian petrol prices above $2.50 per litre in some areas as hundreds of service stations report fuel shortfalls. The government has cut excise by 32 cents per litre and secured supply contracts through May.

Australia holds 39 days of petrol and 29 days of diesel reserves, with rationing triggered if diesel stocks fall to 10 days. More than 90% of Australia's fuel is imported, largely from Asian refineries dependent on Middle Eastern crude that normally transits the Strait of Hormuz.
key_facts: Brent crude above US$111/barrel, Australian prices above $2.50/litre
Hundreds of Australian service stations report fuel shortfalls
Government cut excise by 32 cents per litre
Australia has 39 days petrol, 29 days diesel reserves
sections.australia_relevance: Australia imports over 90% of its fuel from Asian refineries that depend heavily on Middle Eastern crude normally shipped through the Strait of Hormuz.

sections.label: What to Watch
sections.title: Key Developments Ahead
paragraphs: The Israel-Lebanon ceasefire begins at 5pm ET Thursday, though it remains unclear if Hezbollah will comply given its opposition to direct Israeli-Lebanese negotiations. Success of this truce could unlock broader US-Iran talks, with Vice President JD Vance reportedly leading potential negotiations.

Iran continues clearing debris from underground missile bases during the ceasefire, while the US blockade targets Iranian tankers attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz. The ceasefire agreement expires next week, creating urgency for diplomatic breakthrough before potential renewed hostilities.
key_facts: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire effective 5pm ET Thursday for 10 days
Hezbollah compliance uncertain given opposition to talks
Iran clearing debris from missile bases during ceasefire
Current US-Iran ceasefire expires next week
sections.australia_relevance: Fuel price recovery unlikely to be immediate even if Strait reopens, given damaged Middle Eastern refinery infrastructure and depleted global reserves.
